US10 – Layout v2

Channels with axis positioning · Saveable layout presets · Workbooks · Multi-view layout manager

1

Channel model — per-metric axis position + scale

Each metric in a Series panel is a "channel". Channels are independently positioned and scaled within the plot area using percentages (0% = top, 100% = bottom). Channels can overlap or be stacked.

Channel editor

Pace
auto scale
top%
bot%
s/km hi
s/km lo
Heart Rate
auto scale
top%
bot%
bpm hi
bpm lo
Elevation
auto scale
top%
bot%

Quick presets

Live preview

SERIES London 2024 · London 2023 · Manchester · X: distance

Channel bands render in the percentage region you specify. Overlapping bands are drawn with transparency so both are visible.

Channel data model — each channel stores: { metric, yTop: 0–100, yBottom: 0–100, scaleMode: 'auto'|'fixed', scaleMin, scaleMax }. Channels with overlapping y-ranges are rendered with opacity: 0.7 and dashed secondary lines. The x-axis (distance or time) is always shared across all channels.
2

Multi-view layout manager — views, tabs, and the layout grid

The dashboard manages N named "views". The layout grid determines how many are visible at once. Each visible view has its own tab selector to switch its type. Tab mode means only one view is visible — the tab bar at the top IS the view selector.

Mental model: You always have a set of configured views (e.g. "Scatter A", "Series B", "Map C"). The layout manager decides how many to show simultaneously. In Tab mode the tabs at the top switch which single view is visible — exactly like today. In Split or Grid mode, each visible slot has its own mini-tab bar so you can switch that slot's view independently.

Layout = Tab — single view visible, tab bar switches it

StravaViz
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
+ view
Filters
Run · 12 mo3:30–7:00
Roster
London 24
London 23
Scatter plot — 847 activities

Layout = Split — two slots, each with its own in-panel tab selector

StravaViz
Filters
Run · 12 mo
Roster
London 24
London 23
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
Scatter
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
Series (pace + HR channels)

Layout = Grid — 2×2, each slot independently tabbed

StravaViz
Filters
Marathon
Roster
London 24
London 23
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
Scatter
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
Series — Pace+HR
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
Map
Scatter
Series
Map
Table
Table
Key clarification: In Tab mode the top tab bar selects the single visible view — this is identical to today's UX. In Split/Grid mode the top tab bar is replaced by the layout dial, and each panel gets its own tab bar. A user can always switch any slot to any view type without losing the other slots.
3

Layout presets — saveable named configurations

A Layout Preset captures the layout mode (Tab/Split/Grid), which view type fills each slot, and — for Series slots — the full channel configuration (metric, y-position %, scale mode). Managed exactly like filter presets: stored in localStorage, named, loadable instantly.

Layout preset panel

Layout presets
Stored in localStorage as layoutPresets[]. Same save/load/delete pattern as filter presets — reuses the same <details> dropdown component pattern.

What a Layout Preset contains

{
  id:   "lp-race-analysis",
  name: "Race analysis",
  layoutMode: "split",
  slots: [
    {
      viewType: "series",
      channels: [
        { metric: "pace",      yTop: 50, yBottom: 100, scaleMode: "auto" },
        { metric: "heartrate", yTop:  0, yBottom:  50, scaleMode: "fixed",
          scaleMin: 130, scaleMax: 190 }
      ]
    },
    {
      viewType: "map",
      channels: []
    }
  ]
}

A preset captures the full state of the layout — modes, view types, and channel configs — so loading it restores the exact arrangement. Filter state is not included (that belongs to filter presets or Workbooks).

4

Workbooks — named filter × layout combinations

A Workbook is a named configuration combining a filter preset and a layout preset. Loading one instantly applies both. This is the unit of "task" — e.g. "Marathon race review" captures both what data to show and how to visualise it.

Workbook selector (top bar or settings)

Workbooks
Workbooks are stored in localStorage as workbooks[]. A workbook references a filter preset id and a layout preset id by name — it does not duplicate the config, so editing a layout preset updates all workbooks that reference it.

Workbook data model

{
  id:             "wb-marathon-review",
  name:           "Marathon race review",
  filterPresetId: "fp-marathons",
  layoutPresetId: "lp-race-analysis",
  createdAt:      "2026-03-11"
}

Loading flow

1 Look up filterPresetId → apply FilterState to sidebar filter controls
2 Look up layoutPresetId → apply LayoutConfig (mode, slots, channels)
3 Write both to localStorage so next reload restores the Workbook
Open question: Should a Workbook optionally embed the filter/layout config inline (so it stays self-contained even if the underlying preset is deleted)? Or reference by id only (simpler, but brittle)?

Revised type sketch

// Channel — one metric in a Series panel
interface Channel {
  metric:    'pace' | 'heartrate' | 'elevation' | 'cadence'
  yTop:      number   // 0–100 — top of this channel's band (% of plot height from top)
  yBottom:   number   // 0–100 — bottom of this channel's band
  scaleMode: 'auto' | 'fixed'
  scaleMin?: number   // data units (s/km, bpm, m …)
  scaleMax?: number
}

// SlotConfig — what one panel slot shows
interface SlotConfig {
  viewType: 'scatter' | 'series' | 'map' | 'table'
  channels: Channel[]   // only relevant when viewType === 'series'
}

// LayoutPreset — saveable named arrangement
interface LayoutPreset {
  id:         string
  name:       string
  layoutMode: 'tab' | 'split' | 'grid'
  slots:      SlotConfig[]   // length 1 for tab, 2 for split, 4 for grid
}

// Workbook — named filter × layout combination
interface Workbook {
  id:             string
  name:           string
  filterPresetId: string
  layoutPresetId: string
  createdAt:      string
}

Open design questions before implementation

  1. Workbook self-contained vs referenced: embed filter+layout inline in the workbook, or reference by id? Inline is self-contained but duplicates data; by-ref is DRY but breaks if presets are deleted.
  2. Channel yTop/yBottom interpretation: when two channels have overlapping bands (both 0–100%), should they be drawn with transparency + dashed secondary lines, or should there be a hard UI warning?
  3. Scale units: scaleMin/scaleMax are stored in data-layer units (s/km, bpm, metres). Who is responsible for converting when the unit preference changes (km ↔ miles)?
  4. Channels in non-Series views: should Scatter support a secondary colour-encode channel (e.g. colour by HR instead of year)? Out of scope for this story but the channels array on SlotConfig could accommodate it.
  5. Workbook UX entry point: top bar dropdown, settings panel, or a dedicated sidebar section?
  6. Migration: existing sessionStorage scatter/series axis state must map cleanly to the new SlotConfig format on first load.
